notes_md:
**lực** = force, strength, power (physical or abstract). It’s a noun.

It often talks about physical force (lực tay, lực đẩy, lực kéo) or inner strength/energy (nội lực). You’ll also see it in more abstract ideas like **áp lực** (pressure) or **bạo lực** (violence) — same core idea of “force.”

You don’t usually count *lực* by itself; it’s more often part of a compound word. 

Memory tip: think of **lực** as the raw “push power” behind something — your muscles, physics, or even social pressure.

translation.en: It takes a lot of strength to lift the rock.

content.sentence: Cần nhiều ___ để nâng tảng đá.

notes:
lực = force, strength, power (physical or abstract). It’s a noun.

It often talks about physical force (lực tay, lực đẩy, lực kéo) or inner strength/energy (nội lực). You’ll also see it in more abstract ideas like áp lực (pressure) or bạo lực (violence) — same core idea of “force.”

You don’t usually count lực by itself; it’s more often part of a compound word. 

Memory tip: think of lực as the raw “push power” behind something — your muscles, physics, or even social pressure.
exampleSentences: Em cần dùng nhiều lực hơn để mở cái nắp này.
Trong vật lý, anh đang học về các loại lực khác nhau.
Chị ấy mất hết lực sau khi chạy bộ dưới trời nắng.

example.es: Necesitas fuerza para levantar objetos pesados.
example.en: You need strength to lift heavy objects.
example.vi: Cần có lực để nâng vật nặng.

definitions.en: The capacity to exert physical force or energy.
definitions.es: La capacidad de ejercer fuerza o energía física.
definitions.vi: Khả năng tạo ra lực hoặc năng lượng vật lý.

exampleSentencesTranslated: You need to use more force to open this lid.
In physics, I am learning about different types of force.
She lost all her force after jogging under the hot sun.
